summary: Created a planning-only, dependency-ordered M7 Beta content and quality task DAG. The graph covers content lock planning, historical/cultural/language review, full scenario/person/event fill path, large-scale balance and anti-snowball validation, performance/memory/loading validation, Chrome/Edge/Firefox/Windows and resolution/localization matrix, save migration and cloud-save candidate decision boundaries, tutorial/hints/encyclopedia completion, audio/art/localization completion planning, Beta culture review, manual playthrough evidence, formal content lock acceptance, and M7 exit validation without implementing M7 content or product code.

tests_run.result: Modified allowed coordination/status files are present; new untracked M7 task files are present under project/tasks/active. No product code, packages, content-source, tools, CI, roadmap, acceptance matrix, prior gate, ADR, manifest, checksum, or validation artifact paths are modified.
M7-TASK-GRAPH-001 is REVIEW; M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001 is READY but dependency-gated by the REVIEW graph; the other 12 downstream M7 tasks are DRAFT.
No dependency-ready output while M7-TASK-GRAPH-001 remains REVIEW.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON validation for all new M7 task files and DAG acyclicity/dependency existence/status shape
tests_run.result: M7 JSON DAG check ok files=14 edges=27 graphStatus=REVIEW firstReady=M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001 downstreamDraft=12.

tests_run.command: node -e authored changed-path scope guard for M7-TASK-GRAPH-001
tests_run.result: scope guard ok changedPaths=18; changed paths are limited to allowed task/status/message paths and do not include forbidden apps/packages/content-source/tools/CI/docs/gate/roadmap/acceptance-matrix paths.

tests_run.command: node -e UTF-8 BOM check for M7 task files
tests_run.result: M7 task UTF-8 BOM check ok files=14.
No whitespace errors reported before systems handoff creation.
No whitespace errors reported after systems handoff creation.

decisions: Created 13 downstream M7 tasks with dependency order: content lock contract; historical/cultural/language review; scenario/person/event fill; tutorial/hints/encyclopedia; audio/art/localization; Beta culture review; save migration/cloud boundaries; large-scale balance; performance/memory/loading; platform/resolution/localization matrix; manual playthrough evidence; formal content lock acceptance; M7 exit validation.

Set M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001 as the first executable downstream task with status READY; it remains dependency-gated until M7-TASK-GRAPH-001 is accepted/closed. All other downstream M7 tasks remain DRAFT.
Preserved M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS. The graph does not re-describe M6 as unconditional PASS or release readiness.
Preserved DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE exactly. No manual node battle implementation task, M7/M8 blocking spike, research task, validation dependency, or exit-gate dependency was created.

Preserved Human Gates for major cultural-risk decisions, formal content lock acceptance, manual playthrough acceptance where human judgment is required, commercial/release/branding, telemetry, server/multiplayer, cloud-save implementation, arbitrary-code mods, irreversible migration, and frozen product/platform/core changes.

Kept this task planning-only. No apps, packages, content-source, tools, CI, roadmap, acceptance matrix, prior gate docs, accepted ADRs, release artifacts, telemetry, server/multiplayer behavior, arbitrary-code mod support, cloud-save implementation, or manual node battle behavior were modified.
risks: Several downstream M7 tasks are R4 because they later touch content lock, historical/cultural review, save compatibility, platform matrix, large-scale balance, performance, and milestone exit evidence.

Content fill and asset/localization completion can create cultural risk; the DAG routes baseline review and final Beta culture review through historical_researcher and preserves Human Gates for major unresolved risk.
Cloud save is intentionally bounded to candidate decision boundaries; implementation of accounts, network persistence, secrets, telemetry, or provider integration remains outside autonomous M7 work.
Manual playthrough evidence can surface blockers, but human acceptance remains a Human Gate and is not granted by this systems handoff.
requested_action: Perform independent QA review of the M7 task graph for roadmap/acceptance-matrix coverage, dependency ordering, first-ready/downstream-DRAFT status, M6 PASS_WITH_LIMITS preservation, DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE preservation, Human Gate preservation, allowed/forbidden path discipline, architecture-boundary safety, and handoff validity. Accept or request changes.
